# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that creates releases and publishes packages. On version tags, build the application, create a GitHub release, and publish to npm/PyPI. Make the release process fully automated so developers can just push tags to trigger releases.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI/CD
on:
  push:
    tags: true

jobs:
  build_and_publish:
    name: Build and Publish
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v2

      # Python setup.py publish to PyPI
      - if: github.language == 'python'
        name: Setup Python
        run: |
          python3 -m venv .venv
          source .venv/bin/activate
          pip install wheel twine
          pip install -r requirements.txt
          python setup.py sdist bdist_wheel

      # NPM publish to npmjs.com
      - if: github.language == 'node.js'
        name: Setup NodeJS
        run: |
          npm ci
          npm run build

      # Create GitHub release
      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: actions/create-release@v2
        with:
          tag_name: ${{ github.ref }}
          release_name: "${{ github.ref}} Release"
          draft: false
          prerelease: false

      # Publish PyPI package
      - if: github.language == 'python'
        name: Publish PyPI
        run: |
          twine upload dist/*

      # Publish npm package
      - if: github.language == 'node.js'
        name: Publish npm
        run: |
          npm login --registry=https://npm.pkg.github.com
          npm publish
